entry.title: Build Process Automation fails only to send emails within process execution
entry.content:
I have added a process to send an email within a BPA process.

On the BTP Cockpit, we have set Destination to an external SMTP mail service (using Amazon SES), and test mail sending from [Control Tower] -> [Mail Server] in BPA is sending mail without problems.

When I send an email in the process, I am getting an authentication error, but I don't know how to resolve it.
Does the handling of authentication information in Destination change in the process?

Could not send email: The server 'XXXXXXXXXXXXX.amazonaws.com' could be reached, but the login with the given credentials failed. The server has returned the following message: '535 Authentication Credentials Invalid'.

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting- Data Migration & Cutover Approach
entry.content:
Hi massarrat_unwala,

there is no easy answer to it.
With option 2 you can test the conso process and if everything works as planned and you would be able to compare the results against the previous close - especially when no reporting will happen in the old conso tool in April 26.

Option 1 could be quicker,

depending on granularity by doc.typs, you need to consider if doc.types should be reversed or not

- auto. doc.types can not be easily used for Upload so you need to make sure to align it to the migration process (Monitor settings/doc type settings (re-using Auto. Docs or create separate migration docs.), etc.) and changing settings between migration and go live could be tricky (especially, when you are not finished in time with the migration) and could have a negative impact, if you miss something to set back in time.

From my pov the safest way is option 2

I hope this helps

Stephan

entry.title: Aging List report to display overdue amount after currency revaluation
entry.content:
Hello all,

Our company uses 2 FX rates - 

1. Default rate which is used for daily transactions

2. Monthly Average Exchange Rate which is the closing rate for the last day of the month.

The Aging list reports only show the overdue amount using the Default rates but the GL amount is reporting the amount after currency revaluation.

I saw in another forum, we need to use the Accounts Receivable - Open Items report which shows the Amount using Exchange Rate from Foreign Currency Remeasurement of Receivables while Aging List for Receivables report shows the Amount with the Default Exchange Rate.

I would like to know if we can set up the Aging reports to show the amount after Currency Remeasurement?

We need the Aging reports as our auditors are requesting for it.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: 999 storage type difference posting for sto
entry.content:
Hello @ab20468864 

WM movements don't change overall stock levels. Transfer orders just move stock between storage bins, physical or virtual/intermediate ones. 999 is a virtual storage type where stock differences are posted. The differences are created when transfer orders are confirmed with differences or during physical inventory process.

The stock differences need to be cleared periodically with Clearing Differences process. You are missing this step.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Storage Section Check
entry.content:
Hi SAP-Community,

We would like to set up two storage sections (A, B) for one storage type. When creating a putaway warehouse task, the storage type and storage section should be taken into account so that the storage section is displayed in the RFUI during putaway.

The requirement is that a storage bin in storage section B can also be selected despite storage section A being specified. Is this possible without an exception code?

The setting for the Storage Section Check is Y (Storage Section Determination; No Check). I would have expected that this setting allows this. When selecting a storage bin in Storage Section B, we run into the error message "E: Storage bin "" is not assigned to storage section" ( /SCWM/L3088)

In the Storage Section Search Sequence we maintained both A and B (First A, then B) for Storage Section Indicator A. 

What is the difference between Storage Section Check Y and X?

entry.title: Re: variable substitution, filename - remove file extension
Re: No Currency Pair is defined for currencies
entry.content:
Currency exchange rates for the currency pair EUR-USD is defined in the App Currency Exchange Rate

the currency exchange rates can be created and deleted from the app. Also, see what is Direct and Indirect Exchange Rates

For automatic update of exchange rate the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system use the Market Datafeed

Currency notation can be defined in the App Assign Currency Notations - Datafeed

Follow the Set-up instruction for datafeed

Use the App Request Currency Market Data to retrieve the market data into your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.

Please, feel free to reach out to SAP Support with a Support ticket if you are still facing any issue.

 

Kind regards,

Bhawesh

SAP Support

entry.title: Developing a UI5 App with Value Help from CSV, Charts with VizFrame, and Excel Upload/Download
entry.content:
SAPUI5 (SAP User Interface for HTML5) is a JavaScript-based UI framework developed by SAP. It is used for building responsive, cross-platform web applications that run in desktop and mobile browsers. SAPUI5 is the foundation of SAP Fiori, which is SAP’s design language and user experience strategy.

Key Features:

  • MVC Architecture: Supports the Model-View-Controller pattern for clean code separation.

  • Rich UI Controls: Includes a comprehensive set of UI controls optimized for business applications.

  • Data Binding: Supports multiple data models (JSON, XML, OData) for dynamic content rendering.

  • Responsive Design: Adapts to different screen sizes and devices automatically.

  • Theming and Customization: Allows theming via the SAP Theme Designer.

 

What is BAS (Business Application Studio)?

SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) is a modern development environment provided by SAP. It's designed for building business applications, especially those based on SAP technologies like SAPUI5, SAP Fiori, CAP (Cloud Application Programming model), and more.

Think of BAS as SAP's version of Visual Studio Code — but hosted in the cloud and optimized for enterprise application development.

A Dev Space in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) is a pre-configured, isolated development environment tailored for specific types of SAP development — like SAPUI5, CAP, or Fiori.

 

What this app does – simplified scenarios:

  • Show a page titled "Dummy App" with a clean, centered header.

  • Collect user input using a form with the following fields:

    • Name input field with a value help dialog (e.g., a search or selection help).

    • Age input field, also with value help.

    • Company input field, again with value help.

  • Let users submit the form by clicking the "Submit" button, which likely stores or processes the entered data.

  • Display a small bar chart:

    • Shows the number of entries (Count) grouped by Age.

    • Useful for visualizing how many users fall into each age group.

  • Provide a results table that displays the submitted data:

    • Each row shows a person's Name, Age, and Company.

    • Table grows dynamically to show more entries as needed.

  • Allow data export and import:

    • "Download" button lets users export the table data (likely to Excel).

    • "Upload" button lets users import data from a file (probably Excel or CSV).

 

View1.view.xml :

<mvc:View
    controllerName="com.sap.btp.project1.controller.View1"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:viz="sap.viz.ui5.controls"
    xmlns:viz.data="sap.viz.ui5.data"
    xmlns:viz.feeds="sap.viz.ui5.controls.common.feeds"
    xmlns:export="sap.ui.export">

    <!-- Wrap Page inside App to enable scrolling properly -->
    <App>
        <Page id="page" showHeader="false">
            <ScrollContainer height="100%" vertical="true" focusable="true">
                <VBox class="sapUiMediumMargin">

                    <!-- App Header -->
                    <VBox class="appHeader sapUiMediumMargin" alignItems="Center">
                        <Text text="Dummy App" textAlign="Center" class="appTitle" />
                    </VBox>

                    <!-- Main Layout -->
                    <HBox wrap="Wrap">

                        <!-- Form with Value Help Inputs -->
<VBox class="formContainer sapUiSmallMarginEnd">

    <!-- Name Input with Value Help -->
<HBox class="sapUiTinyMarginBottom" alignItems="Center">
    <Label text="Name :" width="100px"/>
    <Input id="nameInput"
           width="300px"
           showValueHelp="true"
           value="{/name}"
           valueHelpRequest="onNameValueHelp" />
</HBox>


   <!-- Age -->
<HBox class="sapUiTinyMarginBottom" alignItems="Center">
    <Label text="Age :" width="100px"/>
    <Input id="ageInput"
           width="300px"
           showValueHelp="true"
           value="{/age}"
           valueHelpRequest="onAgeValueHelp" />
</HBox>

<!-- Company -->
<HBox class="sapUiTinyMarginBottom" alignItems="Center">
    <Label text="Company :" width="100px"/>
    <Input id="companyInput"
           width="300px"
           showValueHelp="true"
           value="{/company}"
           valueHelpRequest="onCompanyValueHelp" />
</HBox>


    <Button text="Submit" press="onSubmit" type="Emphasized" class="sapUiSmallMarginTop" />
</VBox>


                        <!-- Smaller Chart -->
                        <VBox>
                            <viz:VizFrame id="ageChart"
                                          height="250px"
                                          width="400px"
                                          uiConfig="{applicationSet:'fiori'}"
                                          vizType="column">
                                <viz:dataset>
                                    <viz.data:FlattenedDataset data="{/ageChartData}">
                                        <viz.data:dimensions>
                                            <viz.data:DimensionDefinition name="Age" value="{age}" />
                                        </viz.data:dimensions>
                                        <viz.data:measures>
                                            <viz.data:MeasureDefinition name="Count" value="{count}" />
                                        </viz.data:measures>
                                    </viz.data:FlattenedDataset>
                                </viz:dataset>
                                <viz:feeds>
                                    <viz.feeds:FeedItem uid="valueAxis" type="Measure" values="Count"/>
                                    <viz.feeds:FeedItem uid="categoryAxis" type="Dimension" values="Age"/>
                                </viz:feeds>
                            </viz:VizFrame>
                        </VBox>
                    </HBox>

                    <!-- Table & Buttons -->
                    <VBox class="sapUiLargeMarginTop sapUiResponsiveMargin">
                        <HBox justifyContent="End">
                            <Button text="Download"
                                    press="onDownloadExcel"
                                    type="Emphasized"
                                    class="sapUiMediumMarginTop sapUiSmallMarginEnd"/>

                            <Button text="Upload"
                                    press="onUploadFile"
                                    type="Emphasized"
                                    class="sapUiMediumMarginTop"/>
                        </HBox>

                        <Table id="resultTable" items="{/submittedData}" width="100%" growing="true" growingThreshold="20">
                            <columns>
                                <Column><Text text="Name" /></Column>
                                <Column><Text text="Age" /></Column>
                                <Column><Text text="Company" /></Column>
                            </columns>
                            <items>
                                <ColumnListItem>
                                    <cells>
                                        <Text text="{name}" />
                                        <Text text="{age}" />
                                        <Text text="{company}" />
                                    </cells>
                                </ColumnListItem>
                            </items>
                        </Table>
                    </VBox>

                </VBox>
            </ScrollContainer>
        </Page>
    </App>
</mvc:View>
 
XML Namespaces (in SAPUI5, they are often referred to as "UI5 Namespaces").

In an XML-based SAPUI5 view (like View.xml), XML namespaces define which library or module each tag or control belongs to. They prevent naming conflicts and allow the use of multiple libraries in one file.

 

  • xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    ➤ Enables use of MVC tags like <mvc:View> for defining views and controllers.

  • xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    ➤ Provides access to core UI5 controls and features like event handling, formatting, etc.

  • xmlns="sap.m"
    ➤ Default namespace for mobile-ready UI controls like Button, Input, VBox, etc.

  • xmlns:viz="sap.viz.ui5.controls"
    ➤ Gives access to VizFrame chart controls for data visualization.

  • xmlns:viz.data="sap.viz.ui5.data"
    ➤ Used to define datasets, dimensions, and measures for VizFrame charts.

  • xmlns:viz.feeds="sap.viz.ui5.controls.common.feeds"
    ➤ Maps data to chart axes (e.g. valueAxis, categoryAxis) in VizFrame.

  • xmlns:export="sap.ui.export"
    ➤ Provides access to export utilities like Spreadsheet for downloading Excel files.

 

 

Brief Summary of the SAPUI5 View

This SAPUI5 XML View defines a simple Fiori-style web app UI that includes:

  • Header: Displays the title “Dummy App” at the top.

  • Form Section: Allows the user to enter Name, Age, and Company using input fields with value help dialogs (for selection support).

  • Chart: Shows a column chart that visualizes Age-wise count data, using sap.viz.ui5.controls.VizFrame.

  • Data Table: Displays submitted form entries (Name, Age, Company) in a growing table.

  • Buttons:

    • "Submit" to send form data.

    • "Download" to export the table (e.g., to Excel).

    • "Upload" to import data (e.g., from a file).

View1.controller.js:

 

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
    "sap/m/MessageBox",
    "sap/viz/ui5/controls/VizFrame",
    "sap/viz/ui5/data/FlattenedDataset",
    "sap/viz/ui5/data/DimensionDefinition",
    "sap/viz/ui5/data/MeasureDefinition",
    "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel",
    "sap/ui/export/Spreadsheet",
    "sap/m/MessageToast",
    "sap/m/SelectDialog",
    "sap/m/StandardListItem",
    "sap/ui/model/Filter",
    "sap/ui/model/FilterOperator"
], function (
    Controller,
    MessageBox,
    VizFrame,
    FlattenedDataset,
    DimensionDefinition,
    MeasureDefinition,
    JSONModel,
    Spreadsheet,
    MessageToast,
    SelectDialog,
    StandardListItem,
    Filter,
    FilterOperator
) {
    "use strict";

    return Controller.extend("com.sap.btp.project1.controller.View1", {
        onInit: function () {
            var oView = this.getView();
    var oModel = new JSONModel({
        name: "",
        age: "",
        company: "",
        dropdownData: {
            names: [],
            ages: [],
            companies: []
        },
        submittedData: [],
        ageChartData: []
    });
    oView.setModel(oModel);

            var sCSVPath = jQuery.sap.getModulePath("com.sap.btp.project1", "/model/dropdown_master.csv");

            jQuery.ajax({
                url: sCSVPath,
                dataType: "text",
                success: function (data) {
                    var lines = data.split("\n").filter(Boolean);
                    var names = [], ages = [], companies = [];
                    var ageCountMap = {};

                    for (var i = 1; i < lines.length; i++) {
                        var values = lines[i].split(",");
                        if (values.length < 3) continue;

                        var nameVal = values[0].trim();
                        var ageVal = values[1].trim();
                        var companyVal = values[2].trim();

                        names.push({ key: nameVal, text: nameVal });
                        ages.push({ key: ageVal, text: ageVal });
                        companies.push({ key: companyVal, text: companyVal });

                        if (ageVal) {
                            ageCountMap[ageVal] = (ageCountMap[ageVal] || 0) + 1;
                        }
                    }

                    var ageChartData = Object.keys(ageCountMap).map(function (age) {
                        return {
                            age: age,
                            count: ageCountMap[age]
                        };
                    });

                    oModel.setProperty("/dropdownData/names", names);
                    oModel.setProperty("/dropdownData/ages", ages);
                    oModel.setProperty("/dropdownData/companies", companies);
                    oModel.setProperty("/ageChartData", ageChartData);
                },
                error: function () {
                    MessageToast.show("Failed to load dropdown CSV data.");
                }
            });
        },

        // Value Help for Name
        onNameValueHelp: function () {
            var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
            var aNames = oModel.getProperty("/dropdownData/names");

            var oDialog = new SelectDialog({
                title: "Select Name",
                items: aNames.map(function (item) {
                    return new StandardListItem({ title: item.text });
                }),
                liveChange: function (oEvent) {
                    var sValue = oEvent.getParameter("value");
                    var oFilter = new Filter("title", FilterOperator.Contains, sValue);
                    oDialog.getBinding("items").filter([oFilter]);
                },
                confirm: function (oEvent) {
                    var selected = oEvent.getParameter("selectedItem");
                    if (selected) {
                        this.getView().byId("nameInput").setValue(selected.getTitle());
                    }
                }.bind(this)
            });

            oDialog.open();
        },

        // Value Help for Age
        onAgeValueHelp: function () {
            var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
            var aAges = oModel.getProperty("/dropdownData/ages");

            var oDialog = new SelectDialog({
                title: "Select Age",
                items: aAges.map(function (item) {
                    return new StandardListItem({ title: item.text });
                }),
                liveChange: function (oEvent) {
                    var sValue = oEvent.getParameter("value");
                    var oFilter = new Filter("title", FilterOperator.Contains, sValue);
                    oDialog.getBinding("items").filter([oFilter]);
                },
                confirm: function (oEvent) {
                    var selected = oEvent.getParameter("selectedItem");
                    if (selected) {
                        this.getView().byId("ageInput").setValue(selected.getTitle());
                    }
                }.bind(this)
            });

            oDialog.open();
        },

        // Value Help for Company
        onCompanyValueHelp: function () {
            var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
            var aCompanies = oModel.getProperty("/dropdownData/companies");

            var oDialog = new SelectDialog({
                title: "Select Company",
                items: aCompanies.map(function (item) {
                    return new StandardListItem({ title: item.text });
                }),
                liveChange: function (oEvent) {
                    var sValue = oEvent.getParameter("value");
                    var oFilter = new Filter("title", FilterOperator.Contains, sValue);
                    oDialog.getBinding("items").filter([oFilter]);
                },
                confirm: function (oEvent) {
                    var selected = oEvent.getParameter("selectedItem");
                    if (selected) {
                        this.getView().byId("companyInput").setValue(selected.getTitle());
                    }
                }.bind(this)
            });

            oDialog.open();
        },

        onSubmit: function () {
            var oView = this.getView();
            var name = oView.byId("nameInput").getValue();
            var age = oView.byId("ageInput").getValue();
            var company = oView.byId("companyInput").getValue();
       
            var oModel = oView.getModel();
           
            // Make sure these values are set to the model
            oModel.setProperty("/name", name);
            oModel.setProperty("/age", age);
            oModel.setProperty("/company", company);
       
            var validAges = oModel.getProperty("/dropdownData/ages").map(a => a.text);
            var validCompanies = oModel.getProperty("/dropdownData/companies").map(c => c.text);
       
            if (!age || !validAges.includes(age)) {
                MessageBox.error("Please select a valid Age.");
                return;
            }
            if (!company || !validCompanies.includes(company)) {
                MessageBox.error("Please select a valid Company.");
                return;
            }
       
            var message = `Name: ${name}\nAge: ${age}\nCompany: ${company}`;
            MessageBox.information(message, { title: "Submitted Details" });
        },
       

       
        onDownloadExcel: function () {
            var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
            var aData = oModel.getProperty("/submittedData");

            var oExportData = aData.map(function (item) {
                return {
                    "Name": item.name,
                    "Age": item.age,
                    "Company": item.company
                };
            });

            var oSpreadsheet = new Spreadsheet({
                workbook: {
                    columns: [
                        { label: 'Name', property: 'Name' },
                        { label: 'Age', property: 'Age' },
                        { label: 'Company', property: 'Company' }
                    ]
                },
                dataSource: oExportData,
                fileName: "table_data.xlsx"
            });

            oSpreadsheet.build().then(function () {
                MessageToast.show("Excel file has been downloaded!");
            });
        },

        onUploadFile: function () {
            var oInputFile = document.createElement("input");
            oInputFile.type = "file";
            oInputFile.accept = ".xlsx, .xls";
            oInputFile.onchange = this._onFileSelected.bind(this);
            oInputFile.click();
        },

        _onFileSelected: function (oEvent) {
            var oFile = oEvent.target.files[0];

            if (!oFile) {
                MessageToast.show("No file selected.");
                return;
            }

            var loadXLSX = function () {
                return new Promise(function (resolve, reject) {
                    if (typeof XLSX !== "undefined") {
                        resolve();
                        return;
                    }

                    var script = document.createElement("script");
                    script.src = jQuery.sap.getModulePath("com.sap.btp.project1") + "/libs/xlsx.full.min.js";
                    script.onload = resolve;
                    script.onerror = function () {
                        reject(new Error("Failed to load XLSX library."));
                    };
                    document.head.appendChild(script);
                });
            };

            loadXLSX().then(function () {
                var reader = new FileReader();

                reader.onload = function (e) {
                    try {
                        var data = new Uint8Array(e.target.result);
                        var workbook = XLSX.read(data, { type: 'array' });

                        var firstSheetName = workbook.SheetNames[0];
                        var worksheet = workbook.Sheets[firstSheetName];

                        var jsonData = XLSX.utils.sheet_to_json(worksheet);
                        var cleanedData = jsonData.map(function (item) {
                            return {
                                name: item.Name || "",
                                age: item.Age || "",
                                company: item.Company || ""
                            };
                        });

                        var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
                        oModel.setProperty("/submittedData", cleanedData);

                        MessageToast.show("Excel data loaded successfully.");
                    } catch (err) {
                        console.error("Excel parsing error:", err);
                        MessageToast.show("Failed to read Excel file.");
                    }
                }.bind(this);

                reader.readAsArrayBuffer(oFile);
            }.bind(this)).catch(function (err) {
                console.error(err);
                MessageToast.show("Could not load XLSX library.");
            });
        }
    });
});
 

 

SAPUI5 Module Imports & Their Purpose

"sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller"
Base class for all controllers. Used to define your custom controller logic.
"sap/m/MessageBox"
Used to show popup dialog boxes (e.g., for errors, confirmations, info messages).
"sap/viz/ui5/controls/VizFrame"
Core control for rendering charts (bar, pie, line, etc.) in SAPUI5 apps.
"sap/viz/ui5/data/FlattenedDataset"
🔹Defines the data format for charts, mapping flat JSON data to visual elements.
"sap/viz/ui5/data/DimensionDefinition"
Specifies dimensions (e.g., Age) used on the x-axis or category axis of the chart.
"sap/viz/ui5/data/MeasureDefinition"
Specifies measures (e.g., Count) used on the y-axis or value axis of the chart.
"sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel"
Provides a client-side data model using JSON, used to bind data to the UI.
"sap/ui/export/Spreadsheet"
Used for exporting table data to Excel (.xlsx) format.
"sap/m/MessageToast"
Displays temporary messages (toasts) at the bottom of the screen (e.g., “Excel downloaded!”).
"sap/m/SelectDialog"
A searchable dialog used in value help (dropdown) selections.
"sap/m/StandardListItem"
UI element representing a standard item in a list, used inside SelectDialog.
"sap/ui/model/Filter"
Enables filtering of items (e.g., in the value help dialog based on user input).
"sap/ui/model/FilterOperator"
Provides filter operations like Contains, EQ, etc., for filtering logic.
 

Key Functionalities Explained Briefly:

  1. onInit() – Load CSV Data:

    • Loads dropdown_master.csv from the model folder.

    • Extracts Name, Age, and Company options.

    • Stores the values in a JSON model under /dropdownData.

    • Also generates chart data (/ageChartData) based on age counts.

  2. Value Help (SelectDialog) for Each Field:

    • onNameValueHelp(), onAgeValueHelp(), onCompanyValueHelp() open a dialog with search.

    • Items are fetched from the loaded CSV data (/dropdownData).

    • The selected item is set in the respective input field.

  3. onSubmit() – Handle Form Submission:

    • Validates that Age and Company are from valid lists.

    • Shows a message box with the entered Name, Age, and Company.

  4. onDownloadExcel() – Export to Excel:

    • Downloads the /submittedData model (form entries) as an Excel .xlsx file.

  5. onUploadFile() – Upload Excel File:

    • Lets the user upload an Excel file.

    • Reads and parses it using the XLSX library.

    • Updates /submittedData in the model, populating the table in the UI.

 

 

The data shown in the value help comes from a CSV file named dropdown_master.csv, which is located in the model folder inside the webapp directory. This CSV file is loaded as a model and used to populate the dropdown options in the value help dialog.

 

 
In the app, when you click on the value help  icon, a popup dialog opens displaying a list of selectable options. These options—for Name, Age, and Company—are all loaded from a CSV file (dropdown_master.csv) located in the model folder. The same logic applies to each input field.
 
 
 

 

I’ve included a GIF in the demo to visually showcase how the app functions.

 

Learning Materials:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/develop-sapui5-applications 

Learning SAPUI5 for Beginners - SAP Community

Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK

SAP Tutorial Navigator | Tutorials for SAP Developers

entry.title: Block GRPO if Not for A specific Warehouse and A specific Bin
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to create a TN which blocks the addition of GRPO if a specific Bin location is not selected.

I have tried the below TN but it works only for warehouse but not for Bin:

DECLARE @Item AS NVARCHAR(25)
SET @Item = (SELECT TOP 1 ISNULL(T1.ItemCode,'')
FROM OPDN T0
INNER JOIN PDN1 T1 ON T0.DocEntry=T1.DocEntry
INNER JOIN OITM T2 ON T2.ItemCode = T1.ItemCode
INNER JOIN OILM T4 on T1.docentry=T4.DocEntry and T1.LineNum=T4.DocLineNum and T4.TransType=20 and T4.LocCode=T1.WhsCode --AND T4.AccumType=1
LEFT JOIN OBTL T5 on T4.MessageID= T5.MessageID
LEFT JOIN OBIN T6 on T5.BinAbs=T6.absentry and T6.whscode=T1.WhsCode
WHERE T0.DocEntry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del AND T2.QryGroup5 = 'Y'
AND T1.WhsCode NOT IN ('50-QCH') AND T6.BinCode NOT IN( '50-QCH-QC')
)
IF (SELECT @Item) != ''
BEGIN
SET @error = 2003
SET @error_message = (SELECT @Item + ' Item is flagged for Review and must be moved to 50-QCH-QC Bin!!')
GOTO TN_RESULT
END

Any suggestions?

Regards,

Joseph

entry.title: Re: Run Statutory Reports App custom billing document num...
entry.content:
Hello again dear user,

The issue is likely due to the underlying data, not the Excel export format. If only a few billing documents show negative tax amounts, those documents probably involve credit memos, cancellations, or adjustments where the tax is legitimately negative in accounting.

Check the billing type and the related journal entries if the tax was posted as a credit (negative), Excel will just reflect that. It's not a formatting bug, but a reflection of how those specific transactions were processed.

I hope this will help.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Clean Core in SAP S/4HANA: Best Practices for Future-Proof Extensions
entry.content:
No, clean core is way more nuanced, and no goal (or goal summary) in itself.

Side-by-side might be a good solution, for example, built on BTP or on a different (cheaper? better?) platform than BTP. However, that totally depends on the context. Many times I select an in-app(-core) extension for speed, cost and resilience (for example, when side-by-side requires extensive data syncing).

entry.title: Re: Automated tender/RFP management for supplier - multin...
entry.content:
Great question — and you're definitely on the right track considering SAP Ariba.

To break it down:

  1. Finding Tenders/RFPs:
    SAP Ariba (now part of SAP Business Network) lets suppliers get matched with relevant tenders through what used to be called Ariba Discovery. That said, it's mostly buyer-driven, so visibility depends on how actively buyers post opportunities.

  2. Evaluating Opportunities:
    Ariba doesn't offer built-in scoring or evaluation tools for suppliers, but you can work around this by using your own internal tools or integrating it with your CRM or analytics platforms.

  3. Responding/Submitting:
    This is where Ariba works best. You can manage submissions, auto-fill standard info, collaborate internally, and track everything from one place. It's clean, compliant, and built for collaboration.

One thing to keep in mind: Ariba is designed with the buyer in mind. So, for full automation across all three steps, you might want to integrate Ariba with additional tools or workflows.

Happy to chat more if you’re exploring a solution design — this is right in my wheelhouse.

entry.title: UI5 Fast Navigation (F6) and ColumnListItem: Unexpected Behavior When Buttons Are Present
entry.content:
Hello, colleagues!

I’m reaching out to share an accessibility issue I’ve encountered in a project, hoping someone has either faced the same challenge or found a neat workaround.

We are implementing keyboard accessibility support, and part of that involves enabling group skipping using the F6 key — allowing users to move between logical sections or groups of the page, as recommended by SAP accessibility guidelines.
We are using a sap.m.Table with ColumnListItems that represent Appliance Templates. Each row contains some textual information and several action buttons (like Create, Edit, Activate, etc.). Here's the issue:

When pressing F6, instead of jumping from one Appliance Template (i.e., row) to the next, the focus cycles through the buttons inside the first row, then jumps into the buttons of the next row. The expected behaviour would be the following - the Focus should skip from one group (row) to another group, not within internal elements.

 

 

 

 

 

We’ve explored several options to make the ColumnListItem (or its internal layout like HBox) behave as a fast navigation group:

  • Setting data-sap-ui-fastnavgroup="true" via core:CustomData

  • Adding tabindex="0" and role="group"

  • Importing @ui5/webcomponents-base/dist/features/F6Navigation.js (note: relevant mostly for Web Components)

  • Restructuring layout elements like HBox, VBox, OverflowToolbar

  • Removing buttons entirely — and with no buttons, F6 works perfectly

  • Replacing multiple buttons with a single overflow menu button (partially helps)

But none of these worked when the buttons were inside the ColumnListItem.

Thank you in advance! 🙂 

Kind regards,
Silvio

entry.title: Re: Moving Q-stock between Plants
entry.content:
Hello there

You could investigate 'Production in alternative plant' where plant A is your production and plant B is your planning plant (use special procurement 80 as a copy template). GR from production in plant A will go straight into QI stock in plant B. The only "blemish" is that the inspection lot would still be under plant A.

entry.title: Re: How to get notified by email when Application Autosca...
entry.content:
The SAP Alert Notification service for BTP has some standard events about changes/actions in the Cloud Foundry environment. There is a 'process scale' event which is (I think) what you need: https://help.sap.com/docs/alert-notification/sap-alert-notification-for-sap-btp/audit-application-process-scale. Give it a go and let us know if this does the trick?

To set this up, follow the steps here: https://help.sap.com/docs/alert-notification/sap-alert-notification-for-sap-btp/initial-setup and don't forget to activate the 'enableCloudControllerAuditEvents' flag.

entry.title: EWAレポート生成時間について
entry.content:
こちらはノート207223により、EWAをSAP for meで出力設定しました。
しかしABAP上でEWAタスク終わったから、3時間以上経ってSAP for Meでレポート確認できます。

普段はEWAタスク終わってから、SAP社にどのタイミングでSAPForMe側で生成されるのか。
SAP社の設定でない場合は、この生成時間を短縮する方法はありますか、ご教示お願いします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: EWAレポート生成時間について
entry.content:
＞普段はEWAタスク終わってから、SAP社にどのタイミングでSAPForMe側で生成されるのか。
→EarlyWatch Alert (EWA) データは、まずSAP Postboxシステムに送信され、その後、複数のSAP社内システムに複製され、
最終的にSAP Solution Managerシステムで処理されます。処理後、レポートが生成され、EWAアプリケーションにアップロードされる形になります。

＞SAP社の設定でない場合は、この生成時間を短縮する方法はありますか、ご教示お願いします。 
→EWAデータの受信からEWAアプリケーションへのレポートの最終アップロードまでの期間は固定ではありません。
この生成時間を短縮する方法はございません。

以下KBAをご参照ください。
3610055 - How long does it take to generate an EWA report in SAP?

entry.content:
What’s the Contest About?
We’re looking for a fun, original mascot that will represent the spirit of SAP Inside Track Rome – something that blends the innovative SAP world with a touch of Rome’s identity. Think tech meets ancient charm, or cloud meets Colosseum – the possibilities are endless!
The mascot that receives the most Kudos from the community will become the official face of SIT Rome 2025 and will be featured throughout the event.
🏆 The winner will also receive a prize, which will be presented live on October 3, 2025 during the event.

Contest Rules
• Each participant can submit up to 2 mascot designs.
• The mascot must have a clear connection to Rome (culture, landmarks, symbols, etc.).
• Voting will be based on community Kudos – more love, more chances to win!
• Submissions must be received no later than August 31, 2025.
• To participate, post your mascot image(s) in the comments of this blog.

Why Join?
1. Showcase your creativity to the SAP community
2. Contribute to the identity of a global community event
3. Win a special prize and get recognized on stage in Rome!

Don't Forget – Join the Event Too!
Whether you're a designer, speaker, or just an SAP enthusiast, don't miss your chance to be part of this amazing event:
🎤 Call for Speakers is open until July 31, 2025: https://forms.office.com/r/fuQ0bLiahF?origin=lprLink
🎟️ Attendee registration is now open: https://forms.office.com/r/kXXYBd3D6q?origin=lprLink

We can’t wait to see your creativity in action and meet you at #sitRome2025!
Let’s build something special together – with code, ideas, and now… a mascot.

#SAPInsideTrack #sitRome #SAPCommunity #MascotContest #SAPEvents #Rome2025

entry.title: Database level audit requirements
entry.content:
Dear Team,


We have enabled database level auditing for our MSSQL database for INSERT, UPDATE & DELETE queries for all database users.


We are having following further requirements on the auditing part

  1. We need change log/history of all audit trails including old and new values.
  2. We want to activate individual table level database auditing.
  3. We are unable to read generated database audit files. How to read the data captured in these audit files.

Expecting your inputs to achieve the mentioned settings in audit policies.


Thanks,


SAP BASIS Team


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Database level audit requirements
entry.content:
To achieve the requirements for auditing in your MSSQL database, you can follow these steps:

1. Change Log/History of Audit Trails Including Old and New Values

To capture old and new values for INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations, you can use triggers along with auditing. Triggers can help you log the changes into a separate audit table.

Example Trigger for UPDATE:

CREATE TABLE dbo.AuditLog (
    AuditID INT IDENTITY(1,1) PRIMARY KEY,
    TableName NVARCHAR(128),
    Operation NVARCHAR(50),
    OldValue NVARCHAR(MAX),
    NewValue NVARCHAR(MAX),
    ChangedBy NVARCHAR(128),
    ChangedOn DATETIME
);

CREATE TRIGGER trgAuditUpdate
ON dbo.YourTable
AFTER UPDATE
AS
BEGIN
    DECLARE @OldValue NVARCHAR(MAX), @NewValue NVARCHAR(MAX);

    SELECT @OldValue = (SELECT * FROM deleted FOR JSON PATH, WITHOUT_ARRAY_WRAPPER);
    SELECT @NewValue = (SELECT * FROM inserted FOR JSON PATH, WITHOUT_ARRAY_WRAPPER);

    INSERT INTO dbo.AuditLog (TableName, Operation, OldValue, NewValue, ChangedBy, ChangedOn)
    VALUES ('YourTable', 'UPDATE', @OldValue, @NewValue, SUSER_SNAME(), GETDATE());
END;

Example Trigger for INSERT:

CREATE TRIGGER trgAuditInsert
ON dbo.YourTable
AFTER INSERT
AS
BEGIN
    DECLARE @NewValue NVARCHAR(MAX);

    SELECT @NewValue = (SELECT * FROM inserted FOR JSON PATH, WITHOUT_ARRAY_WRAPPER);

    INSERT INTO dbo.AuditLog (TableName, Operation, OldValue, NewValue, ChangedBy, ChangedOn)
    VALUES ('YourTable', 'INSERT', NULL, @NewValue, SUSER_SNAME(), GETDATE());
END;

Example Trigger for DELETE:

CREATE TRIGGER trgAuditDelete
ON dbo.YourTable
AFTER DELETE
AS
BEGIN
    DECLARE @OldValue NVARCHAR(MAX);

    SELECT @OldValue = (SELECT * FROM deleted FOR JSON PATH, WITHOUT_ARRAY_WRAPPER);

    INSERT INTO dbo.AuditLog (TableName, Operation, OldValue, NewValue, ChangedBy, ChangedOn)
    VALUES ('YourTable', 'DELETE', @OldValue, NULL, SUSER_SNAME(), GETDATE());
END;

2. Individual Table Level Database Auditing

To activate auditing at the individual table level, you can create audit specifications for each table.

Example:

-- Create a server audit
CREATE SERVER AUDIT MyServerAudit
TO FILE (FILEPATH = 'C:\AuditLogs\MyAuditLogs');

-- Enable the server audit
ALTER SERVER AUDIT MyServerAudit WITH (STATE = ON);

-- Create a database audit specification for a specific table
CREATE DATABASE AUDIT SPECIFICATION MyDatabaseAuditSpec
FOR SERVER AUDIT MyServerAudit
ADD (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON dbo.YourTable BY [public]);

-- Enable the database audit specification
ALTER DATABASE AUDIT SPECIFICATION MyDatabaseAuditSpec WITH (STATE = ON);

3. Reading Generated Database Audit Files

To read the data captured in the audit files, you can use the sys.fn_get_audit_file function.

Example:

SELECT *
FROM sys.fn_get_audit_file('C:\AuditLogs\MyAuditLogs\*.sqlaudit', DEFAULT, DEFAULT);

This function reads the audit files and returns the audit records. You can filter and process these records as needed.

entry.title: Note-Oriented Discovery of Maintenance Planner for SAP Security Notes
entry.content:
With the May release, SAP has rolled out the new feature Note-Oriented Discovery in the Maintenance Planner.

This feature offers an option within the search interface for analyzing the SAP Security Notes that need to be implemented in the systems in your responsibility. It covers ABAP, Java, and HANA systems.

For example, a list of all productive SIDs and the Security Notes with a CVSS score > 9.5 that still need to be implemented can be generated:

Furthermore, is is also possible to search for all SIDs where a specific SAP Security Note is still pending implementation by entering the note number in the search mask. This can be helpful to gat a quick overview in case a high severity vulnerability must be fixed and the implementation status is to be tracked. Another use case could be to gain insight in very large, distributed landscapes with multiple SolMans/FRUNs or if service providers are involved.

Preconditions

One precondition is that all systems send telemetry data to the SAP Support Backbone.

To identify whether all system's data is recent, you can navigate in the Maintenance Planner to Explore Systems. The systems sending telemetry data are listed with their 'Last Synchronized On' (last uploaded to LMDB from SLD) and ' Last Replicated on' (last updated in Maintenance Planner - I assume only updated if there have been changes) date or even with an 'Outdated' indicator below the SID:

If the data is not current, please verify the process of data sending depending on your scenario, either via SolMan, FRUN, or directly.

Spoiler
For the ones interested in the details of sending data to the SAP Support Backbone, I recommend reading https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/system-data-upload-to-the-sap-support-portal-via-landscape-information/ba-p/13452237. 

Furthermore, in the data collection, it must be ensured the SAP Note implementation status (Note number and implemented version) is also collected and sent to SAP. For this

  1. Go to transaction RZ70.
  2. Click 'Show/Hide Details'.
  3. Make sure '_SLD_INSTCORR' is enabled.
  4. For '_SLD_INSTCORR', set the flag in column Display and click 'Start SLD Data Collection Now' and verify if a list of notes is show. If not, go back to RZ70 and click 'Proposal' next to the list of Data Collection Programs' and repeat the test.
  5.  For '_SLD_INSTCORR', remove the flag in column Display.
  6. It should look like this:

Big thanks to @huberda for verifying the whole use case and coming up lightning fast with the solution for missing SAP Note data!

 

This new feature brings us one step closer to get features replaced before the EoL of the SAP Solution Manager.

The business requirement is to have these column labels change dynamically, showing the respective characteristic’s description. This information is available in backend tables. Whenever characteristics are added or removed in TVARC table in the backend, the corresponding List Report table columns’ labels should reflect the updated descriptions instead of generic labels like "Batchar1", "Batchar2", and so on.

To illustrate the scenario more clearly, I’ve attached a model screenshot. Here the label for "Placeholder1" column in Table 1 should be picked from "Description" column in Table 3.

 Ex. Placeholder1 becomes “Label1” and Placeholder2 becomes “Label2”

 

I would like to kindly request your input and guidance on how feasible it would be to implement such dynamic column labelling in this context.

Thanks,

Ilkin

entry.title: Re: Challenge in Assigning admin role to business user in...
entry.content:
Hello,

Steps to Assign Admin Role:

  1. Access SAP Fiori Launchpad:

    • Ensure you are logged into the SAP Fiori Launchpad using appropriate credentials.

  2. Navigate to ‘Maintain Business Roles’:

    • Open the Maintain Business Roles app. Creating a Business Role and Assigning Business Users (Administrator)This app allows you to create or modify business roles.

  3. Create or Select Business Role:

    • If you haven’t created a business role for administrators yet, use the option â€˜Create from Templateâ€™ to select a suitable template.
    • Choose, for instance, SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR as the template.

  4. Assign Users to Role:

    • Once the business role is created, navigate to the ‘Assigned Business Users’ tab.
    • Use the Add button to search and assign the business user you want to provide admin access to.

  5. Review Permission Settings:

    • Ensure that the permissions for Read, Write, and Value Help are set to Unrestricted, especially if creating a new role.

  6. Save Changes:

    • Once changes are made, save them to ensure the business user has been assigned the correct role.

Troubleshooting Tips:

  • Ensure Proper System Access: Verify that you have access to the test environment as required by your learning journey.
  • Check Authorization: Sometimes, issues might occur if the account you’re using doesn’t have the right permissions to make changes.
  • Re-login: Occasionally, a simple re-login to the SAP Fiori Launchpad can resolve issues if the interface isn’t responding.

If you cannot find the Maintain Business Users App, you may not have the proper roles. If you click on the app but the app does not open, it could be your system has errors. You may try in a different system.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors HCM: Highlights from SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
Top Takeaways for HR Leaders from SAP Sapphire 2025

SAP Sapphire 2025 delivered a bold message, the future of Human Capital Management (HCM) is intelligent, agile, and AI-powered. With new product launches, strategic pivots, and major updates across analytics, service delivery, and digital adoption, SAP is leading the next evolution of HR transformation.

These innovations draw on SAP’s unparalleled integration of business data and enterprise-grade AI - helping HR leaders make smarter decisions, deliver better experiences, and connect people strategy to business success like never before.

Discover what’s next in human capital management and what it means for your team.

Key Innovations in SAP SuccessFactors HCM

1. People Intelligence in SAP Business Data Cloud

Announced during Dan Beck’s HCM keynote, People Intelligence is SAP's next-generation people analytics platform, now integrated with the SAP Business Data Cloud. This solution unifies people and skills data from SAP SuccessFactors with broader business datasets, turning it into actionable, AI-powered insights for HR and business leaders.

Highlights:

  • Self-service, business-friendly interface

  • Hundreds of HR KPIs and AI-based forecasts

  • Governed, enriched workforce data for decision-making

  • Joule copilot integration for instant answers

People Intelligence shall go General Availability sometime in Q3 2025.
Target audience: EC + Workforce Analytics customers aligning with SAP's analytics stack.

2. Performance and Goals Agent

The new Performance and Goals Agent is designed to supercharge the employee-manager relationship. The Performance and Goals Agent aims to enhance performance management by equipping managers with the information they need to have productive and insightful conversations with their team members. 

 
Functionality:
The agent utilizes data-driven insights to help managers identify areas for improvement, highlight employee strengths, and tailor development plans.

Availability:
While the agent was announced at SAP Sapphire 2025, its general availability is planned for Q4 2025. 

3. WalkMe for SAP SuccessFactors HCM

In collaboration with WalkMe, SAP SuccessFactors now features AI-powered in-app guidance across key workflows like onboarding and time-off requests.

Key capabilities:

  • Click-by-click user journey tracking

  • AI-powered step-by-step support and automation

  • Built-in guides for onboarding, compliance, and training

  • Customizable packages for enterprise-wide digital adoption

Availability: Embedded by default effective 1H 2025, with upgrade options to Platform and Premium WalkMe licenses.

4. SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management

Replacing the legacy ECSC (Employee Central Service Center), this new solution delivers purpose-built, AI-powered HR case management and self-service. This new solution brings AI-based case management and self-service HR capabilities into one unified experience.

Features:

  • Intelligent case routing and prioritization with Joule AI

  • Customized case flows for complex HR scenarios

  • Proactive support and sentiment analysis

  • Seamless integration with Employee Central master data

Availability: SKU is now active. ECSC customers will need reimplementation; migration tools and support are in development.

5. New SAP Business Suite Packages for HCM

To further accelerate transformation, SAP is introducing modular SAP Business Suite Packages for HCM. These are pre-integrated bundles of HR capabilities built on SAP BTP that simplify implementation and reduce time-to-value.

These packages provide integrated solutions across:

  • Core HR

  • Workforce planning and analytics

  • HR service delivery

  • AI and automation

Packages allow HR teams to:

  • Choose capabilities based on current needs
  • Scale and integrate easily over time
  • Leverage the full power of SAP Business AI and analytics

Ideal for CHROs looking for a fast, scalable route to value with deep integration and extensibility.

Key Takeaways for HR Leaders

  • AI that Drives Impact: SAP’s embedded AI enables HR teams to make decisions faster and with confidence.
  • Smarter, More Engaged Employees: Tools like WalkMe and the Performance Agent create better experiences and improve adoption.
  • Data-Driven Workforce Strategy: People Intelligence empowers leaders to align skills, roles, and resources with business strategy.
  • Faster, Simpler Transformation: SAP’s modular HCM packages reduce complexity and help teams move quickly from planning to value realization.

Final Thoughts

Your best. Made real. Powered by SAP SuccessFactors.

In an era marked by constant change and workforce uncertainty, SAP’s latest HCM innovations offer a roadmap for resilient, data-driven HR transformation.

By combining AI, people insights, and enterprise integration, SAP SuccessFactors continues to set the benchmark for modern HCM — helping organizations around the world not only keep up, but lead the way forward.

For a complete view of all innovations announced at SAP Sapphire 2025, visit the:
SAP Sapphire 2025 Innovation Guide

SAP Sapphire 2025 continues in:

  • Madrid: May 26–28

entry.title: Re: (APIs) TransportationCode -1 not acquired
entry.content:
Hi dausto,

If you would like to keep the same value on Lines, you will have to update the document twice.

In the first update, set value on the header as Null and change the value on lines to some other value as below:

PATCH /b1s/v1/Orders(1234)
{
    "TransportationCode": null,
    "DocumentLines": [
        {
            "LineNum": 0,
            "ShippingMethod": 2
        }
    ]
}

In the second update, set the desired value on the lines.

PATCH /b1s/v1/Orders(1234)
{
    "TransportationCode": null,
    "DocumentLines": [
        {
            "LineNum": 0,
            "ShippingMethod": 3
        }
    ]
}

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Resolving Carrier Mismatch Errors When planning Freight Units within SAP Transportation Management
entry.content:
Transportation is a complex process, often requiring careful coordination of Transportation planners and various supply chain partners. This may require the Transportation planners to replan / reassign the demand (Freight Units) to the capacity (Freight order / Trucks). At times, one issue that can arise is a carrier mismatch error when trying to reassign Freight Units (FUs) to Freight Orders (FOs) after they have been unplanned. In this blog, we will explore this issue and offer some possible options to avoid this issue.

Let’s first look at the details of this issue.

When Freight Units are unplanned from an existing Freight Order, they become visible as unplanned in the SAP Transportation Management cockpit. However, attempting to reschedule them into a different freight order can result (under certain conditions) in a carrier mismatch error. This issue arises when the carrier for the freight units is "derived from predecessor documents," creating a discrepancy if the new Freight Order has a different carrier.

Here is a typical process that leads to this error:

1. Create a Sales order and a Freight Unit (FU) and, using the transportation cockpit, create a Road Freight Order (FO) based on the FU.
2. Open the FO and assign a carrier to it. Save your changes.
3. Delete the FO using the appropriate option within the Transportation Cockpit.
4. Next, create a new, empty FO with a different carrier and attempt to assign the initial FU to the newly created FO by selecting both and clicking "Assign Selected Items.”
5. Save the assignment process and encounter no error at this point.

However, the process can also take place when you have a Delivery created. Here the Freight unit is planned on the FO and the carrier may be assigned either to the delivery or to the Freight Order. Now, when the associated FU is unplanned from the FO, the carrier information remains attached to both the FU and the delivery. As you can see in the below screenshot it mentions “Derived from Predecessor Documents”.

At this point if an assignment is planned to a new FO with a different carrier it leads to the system giving an error “Freight unit xxxx cannot be assigned to Freight Order yyyy, carrier doesn't match”.

The system's retention of the carrier information can be due to various scenarios, such as the carrier being inherent in the delivery and thus needing to be retained despite the unplanning of Freight Order and Freight Unit. There can also be a scenario where the transportation planner may only be changing the Freight Order / Truck and continuing with the existing carrier.

To overcome this challenge, one might consider the following strategies

  1. When Carrier was assigned within Freight order
    1. Remove the Carrier from FO1 Before Unplanning / Deletion: By clearing the carrier assignment before unplanning / deleting the FO, you ensure that the FU / delivery has no carrier association and can be reassigned freely.
    2. Assign Carrier After Planning: Once you have unplanned the FU and need to reassign it, place it on a FO without a carrier and then assign the carrier to FO post-planning.
  2. When Carrier was assigned within Delivery
    1.  Delete Carrier from Delivery: Ensure that after FO Unplanning / deletion, the FU and delivery have no carrier specified. This allows reassignment to another FO without issues.

While carrier mismatch is a standard behavior that can occur due to multiple scenarios (some of which are mentioned above), understanding the underlying system logic and utilizing these strategies can facilitate smoother freight unit planning and assignment processes. Kindly note that there can be other options also that can be explored.

Should these solutions not address all nuances of your case, consider submitting a feature request on the [SAP Customer Influence Portal] (https://me.sap.com/notes/0002963059).

entry.title: SAP Market rates management - multiple sources
entry.content:
Hi experts,

we are planning to implement the SAP Market Rates Management – Bring Your Own Rates (BYOR) solution to enable the automatic upload and integration of market rates into our client's SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public system. Before we implement the solution, our interest lies in understanding whether the product's functionalities will support uploading currency exchange type M from 3 different sources based on the currency pair?

E.G. if the pair will be:

- Any currency to EUR - source will be ECB

- Any currency to RON - source will be National bank of Romania

- Any currency to CZK - source will be Czech national bank

Thank you in advance,
JB

entry.title: Change Process in CPQ – BRIM S/4 Solution Quotation Integration
entry.content:
Change is the only constant thing is a common knowledge, so how can business documents be an exception to this? It is not! We live in constantly evolving dynamic times, where business strategies, negotiations and deals are also being adapted and changed based on multitude of influencing factors. So, with every change, how the change is managed and how we execute and plan for change especially when it comes to contractual documents like Subscription contract is the topic for this blog.

The CPQ – BRIM integration enables SAP customers to have the best of on cloud CPQ for quoting process and on-prem BRIM for follow-on contract document creation to further proceed with the quote to cash scenario for revenue generation.  As part of contract lifecycle management, for subscription products, this process further extends to support changes to existing subscription contracts. These changes can be made to existing contracts or new contracts can be created for the changes triggered from an existing contract.

Changes like contract extension, renewal, cancellation, change in quantity/ price/configuration adding new products, swapping existing product with new products etc. are possible. Only specific change processes are available based on the state of the contract, e.g. cancelled contract cannot be extended or renewed.

Apart from BRIM prerequisites and configurations,  administrators need to enable access rights for the Assets Table Management and Change Processes sections as explained in Assign Access Rights for Setup Sections and Entities , else the sections won’t be visible in the Setup.

How subscription contract change-process works?

The term Change Process here refers to a singular change that can be executed on a subscription contract document. These Change Processes when group together based on business need and flexibility required form a group of processes called Change Process Groups. A single Change Process can belong to multiple change process groups. Example we have change process groups like Change Quantity and Price, where Change Quantity and Change Price are grouped together. Change Process Group column is synced from S/4 HANA, it can be renamed as per required. This column is reflected in the Assets section of the quote page. Kindly note not all change process in S/4 HANA are supported in this integration. Depending on the change process/group selected in the Asset Section, the subsequent fields in the product section become editable. The editability of the fields can be controlled from the Set-up section. From the above example, the quantity and price can be set to editable.

 

Special change process features like New Contract, Co-Term and Swap, must be additionally set for specific change process groups to further control the and align the behaviour to organization needs. New contract enables the sales rep to either create a new contract from the existing contracts or merge all contracts into one. On placing the order, the new contract is created in SAP S/4 HANA. The old contracts are cancelled, and the references are available in SAP S/4 HANA subscription contract document.

 

To change a particular Subscription contract or to be more specific the items of a subscription contract, we must add it to the quote via the Add Assets “+” icon. These items are termed as Assets and added to the Asset section of the quote. This Asset section can also be personalised via the set-up. The subscription contracts tied to the selected Involved Party in Quotes page are available to be added to quote as Assets.

 

 

 

After adding Assets to the quote page, if the toggle in the quote header section is turned ON to create a new subscription contract, the subsequent changes to the assets selected from one or more contracts will be transferred to a new contract starting on the day of initiating the changes and the existing contracts will be discontinued.

Mostly every change process has direct impact price or value of the quote. Contract Change Activation Date is the reference taken for adjusting all quote relevant price calculations so that prices are always in line with the changes and applicable from the date changes are implemented.

To conclude, for subscription products the SAP CPQ – BRIM integration offers great flexibility to change the already created subscription contracts with feasibility to group multiple changes, change multiple contracts at the same time create new contracts, merge contracts, cancel contracts etc. to enabling smooth management of the contract lifecycle.

Summary Settings for change process

SAP CPQ Set-UpSAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration/iflows)Solution Quotation in S/4 HANA BRIM
Quote Layout : Add Asset SectionRead Change Process Groups for Quote from SAP BRIMSubscription Contracts
Quotes -Assets Table Management : Configure the view of Asset TableRead Change Process for Quote from SAP BRIMChange Processes
Workflow: Preparing – Preparing: Action – Add Asset to Quote and other actionsRead allowed Change Process Groups for Subscription Contract Item from SAP BRIMChange Process Group
Quotes- Change Processes – Add Change Process, group and give relevant names to the group. Additional settings can be maintainedRead Subscription contract header from BRIM 
 Read Subscription contract items from BRIM 
 Replicate Change Contract Processing Status from SAP S4HANA 

 

Most used Change Process Capabilities

Sno

 Change Processes/ Capabilities

Description

Additional Info

1

New Contract

Enables to renew or change the existing subscription contract and create a new contract with reference to it.

On placing the order, the new/merged contract is created in SAP S/4 HANA. The old contracts are cancelled, and the references are available in SAP S/4 HANA subscription contract document.

2

Merge

Enables consolidation of multiple contracts into one contract.

3

Product Change/Swap

Enables the change/replacement of an existing subscription product in a contract.

In Set up -Quote -Change Processes -Change Process Group Tab, for the selected change process group, you must select the values for additional settings to Swap.

4

Co-Term

Enables user to co-term – adjust to the same contract duration for different items in a contact.

 

5

Cancellation

Enables users to cancel a contract item.

Cancellation process needs to be set-up.

6

Contract Extension

Enables the extension of the contact term as set-up in product master data).

 

7

Change Item Quantity

Enables users to change the item quantity for products in a contract.

 

8

Change Configuration 

Enables users to modify the variant configuration-based characteristics for the configurable products in a contract.

 

9

Change Price

Enables users to change the price of the products in the contract

 

10

Add Sub-item

Enables the addition of sub-items during change processes

 

 

Disclaimer: Given the dynamic nature of technology and features, and the potential limits to the author's knowledge, please consider the contents of this blog as an attempt to simplify the subject matter and disseminate further information about it. Therefore, it is recommended to verify the details from official sources or conduct further research to ensure complete accuracy and up-to-date information. The insights provided are meant to foster learning and discussion, not to serve as definitive guidance.
